name: Cinnamon-Nut-Raisin Crescents (Rugelach)
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Cream cheese (bar-type, not soft type)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the cream cheese into tablespoon-sized pieces and let soften at room temperature.
2. Cut the butter into small pieces of about 1/2 tbsp. and keep cold until ready to use.
3. In a food processor, combine flour, salt, and butter. Process with on/off turns until mixture resembles coarse meal.
4. Add cream cheese and sour cream, distributing them fairly evenly over the mixture. Process with on/off turns until dough just holds together.
5. If necessary, add 1 teaspoon water.
6. Wrap the dough in a clean cloth and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or up to 2 days. Lightly butter two or three baking sheets.
7. In a small bowl, mix sugar and ground cinnamon for filling.
8. Divide the dough into four pieces. Press one piece of dough into a round shape, then flatten it. Roll it out to a 9-inch circle about 1/8-inch thick.
9. Sprinkle 1/4 of the sugar-cinnamon mixture (about 2 tbsp.) over the circle, and sprinkle 1/4 of nuts and raisins around the outer edge of the circle.
10. Press with a rolling pin so the filling adheres to the dough.
11. Using a heavy knife, cut the circle into 12 wedges with sharp, smooth cuts from the wide end to the point.
12. Roll up each dough piece tightly from the wide end to the point, ensuring that the filling is enclosed. The raisins should not be exposed to prevent burning.
13. Repeat the process for the remaining three pieces of dough.
14. Place the cookies on the baking sheets with the points of triangles facing down, spacing them about 1 inch apart.
15. Refrigerate the cookies while shaping more if needed.
16. Bake in a preheated oven at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 22 to 25 minutes, or until light golden. Allow to cool on wire racks.
Yields: 48 small cookies
